chapter_title.en: Chapter 106 – Selling the juice and the wine

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/276)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/276)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ نَصْرَانِيٍّ أَسْلَمَ وَ عِنْدَهُ خَمْرٌ وَ خَنَازِيرُ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ هَلْ يَبِيعُ خَمْرَهُ وَ خَنَازِيرَهُ وَ يَقْضِي دَيْنَهُ قَالَ لَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a Christian who became a Muslim and with him was wine and pigs, and upon him were debts. Can he sell his wine and his pigs and fulfil his debts?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No’.
en.sarwar: 14. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from certain persons of our people who has said the following: “I once asked al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a Christian man who became a Muslim and had wine and pigs and owed debts; if he could sell his wine and pigs to pay off his debts. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, he cannot do so.’”
